title: Sewing machinists
description: All installation, maintenance, and repair workers not listed separately. Perform technical activities at power plants or individual installations necessary for the generation of power from geothermal energy sources. Monitor and control operating activities at geothermal power generation facilities and perform maintenance and repairs as necessary. Install, test, and maintain residential and commercial geothermal heat pumps. Operate or tend sewing machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or perform related sewing operations in the manufacture of garment or nongarment products. (Based on 13 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Operate or tend machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or finish shoes, shoe parts, and garment articles, using hand, foot, or knee controls, and monitor machine operations to detect any problems such as defective stitching or machine malfunctions.
Select and place spools of thread or pre-wound bobbins into machines, draw thread through machine guide slots, and fill shuttle spools with thread from bobbins in preparation for sewing tasks.
Align parts to be stitched, selecting appropriate supplies such as fasteners and thread, and inspecting finished products to ensure quality and adherence to specifications.
Perform routine maintenance on sewing machines, including cleaning, lubricating, and replacing parts such as needles, and notify supervisors of mechanical malfunctions as necessary.
Cut excess thread or material from finished products, trim edges, and mark or discard items with defects or unsatisfactory finishes.
Sew, join, reinforce, or finish parts of garments and other articles using various sewing techniques, and monitor and adjust machine controls for heating, tension, and speed as needed.
Measure and align parts, applying specified techniques such as pressing or felling, and check designs against specifications to verify conformity and quality standards.
Study work orders or other instructions for information about workloads, processes, and required materials, and confer with coworkers to resolve issues that arise during production.
Position items under needles while using guides or templates to ensure accurate stitching, and adjust settings such as stitch length and thread tension accordingly.
Create, modify, and critique patterns according to specifications, ensuring material yield optimization and minimizing waste during the cutting and sewing process.
Transport items to and from work areas as needed, and keep accurate records of materials processed and work completed during production.
Inspect machinery and products for defects, performing necessary adjustments and repairs to ensure proper operation and adherence to quality standards.
Utilize various hand tools and equipment to cut, trim, and prepare materials for sewing, such as marking out cutting lines and shaping items as required.

Operate or tend sewing machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or perform related sewing operations in the manufacture of garment or nongarment products, including constructing, decorating, or repairing leather products, and performing tasks such as aligning and stitching materials and repairing or replacing soles, heels, and other parts of footwear using sewing equipment and techniques.

Identify and correct malfunctions of equipment, prepare and maintain logs of work performed, monitor production operations or equipment to ensure conformance to specifications, and perform inspections of articles for defects, taking necessary corrective actions.
Read blueprints, manuals, or other materials to determine specifications and adjust, clean, or repair products or processing equipment to correct defects found during inspections.

Collect and record data associated with products, analyze test data, including computing defect percentages or averages, and prepare inspection reports describing results and recommendations for needed repairs.
Determine materials needed for projects, select patterns, and trace or draw patterns onto materials for cutting.
Clean, maintain, and calibrate measuring instruments to ensure accuracy, and inspect or test raw materials to ensure compliance with standards.
Monitor machines for sorting or inspecting products, and backfill piping trenches to protect installed systems in the sewing machine context where applicable.
Read prescriptions or specifications to establish product requirements, and consult with customers to estimate costs or needed repairs.
Attach accessories or ornamentation to products, and prepare components, such as heel pads and lifts, based on customer specifications or requirements.
Dye, polish, paint, or otherwise finish leather or materials to obtain desired effects or decorations, including dressing boots or shoes and shaping shoe heels.
Fabricate, install, position, or connect components for testing or operational purposes.
